categories.introduction: The Fruit Ontology catalogs canonical culinary fruits, berries, citrus, melons, and fruit-derived ingredients used worldwide in cooking. This domain models culinary ingredients, not horticulture, beverages, or prepared foods.
categories.name: Fruits
categories.seo_description: Explore canonical culinary fruits and berries by group — usage intensity, identity, and pairing metadata in the PairingMethod Food Ontology.
categories.summary: Canonical culinary fruits across seven immutable groups: Pomes, Stone Fruits, Citrus, Berries, Tropical Fruits, Melons, and Processed Fruits.

groups.introduction: Pomes in the Fruit Ontology — core-bearing fruits used fresh and in cooking, including apple and pear.
groups.name: Pomes
groups.seo_description: Canonical pome fruits — apple, pear, and related culinary ingredients in the PairingMethod Fruit Ontology.
groups.summary: Pomes — canonical pome fruits in the Fruit Ontology (population deferred to FOOD-09B).

groups.introduction: Stone fruits in the Fruit Ontology — drupes with fleshy mesocarp and hard endocarp, including peach, plum, cherry, and apricot.
groups.name: Stone Fruits
groups.seo_description: Canonical stone fruits — peach, plum, cherry, and related culinary ingredients in the PairingMethod Fruit Ontology.
groups.summary: Stone Fruits — canonical stone fruits in the Fruit Ontology (population deferred to FOOD-09B).

groups.introduction: Citrus in the Fruit Ontology — acidic, aromatic fruits used for juice, zest, segments, and cooking, including lemon, lime, orange, and grapefruit.
groups.name: Citrus
groups.seo_description: Canonical citrus fruits — lemon, lime, orange, and related culinary ingredients in the PairingMethod Fruit Ontology.
groups.summary: Citrus — canonical citrus fruits in the Fruit Ontology (population deferred to FOOD-09B).

groups.introduction: Berries in the Fruit Ontology — small, aggregate, and culinary berry-class fruits used fresh and in desserts and sauces, including strawberry, raspberry, blueberry, and blackberry.
groups.name: Berries
groups.seo_description: Canonical berries — strawberry, raspberry, blueberry, and related culinary ingredients in the PairingMethod Fruit Ontology.
groups.summary: Berries — canonical berries in the Fruit Ontology (population deferred to FOOD-09B).

groups.introduction: Tropical fruits in the Fruit Ontology — warm-climate fruits with distinct culinary identities, including mango, pineapple, papaya, banana, and coconut.
groups.name: Tropical Fruits
groups.seo_description: Canonical tropical fruits — mango, pineapple, coconut, and related culinary ingredients in the PairingMethod Fruit Ontology.
groups.summary: Tropical Fruits — canonical tropical fruits in the Fruit Ontology (population deferred to FOOD-09B).

groups.introduction: Melons in the Fruit Ontology — high-water fruits used fresh and in cold preparations, including watermelon, cantaloupe, and honeydew.
groups.name: Melons
groups.seo_description: Canonical melons — watermelon, cantaloupe, honeydew, and related culinary ingredients in the PairingMethod Fruit Ontology.
groups.summary: Melons — canonical melons in the Fruit Ontology (population deferred to FOOD-09B).

groups.introduction: Processed fruits in the Fruit Ontology — dried fruits, fruit purées, and fruit pastes with established independent culinary identities governed by FRUIT-001 and PROC-001, including raisin, prune, and date.
groups.name: Processed Fruits
groups.seo_description: Canonical processed fruit ingredients — raisins, prunes, dates, and related culinary forms in the PairingMethod Fruit Ontology.
groups.summary: Processed Fruits — canonical processed fruit ingredients in the Fruit Ontology (population deferred to FOOD-09B).

meta.description: Fruit Ontology — governance shell only (FOOD-09A). Entity population deferred to FOOD-09B.

aroma_profile.description: Reserved aroma characteristic tags — vocabulary frozen in FOOD-09A; do not populate until post-FOOD-09B curation standards.

canonical_culinary_groups.description: Immutable group names and slugs — do not rename or add peer groups in v1.

culinary_group.description: Canonical Culinary Group value on leaf entities — aligns to immutable group slugs.

flavor_profile.description: Reserved flavor characteristic tags — vocabulary frozen in FOOD-09A; do not populate until post-FOOD-09B curation standards.

texture_profile.description: Reserved texture characteristic tags — vocabulary frozen in FOOD-09A; do not populate until post-FOOD-09B curation standards.

usage_intensity.description: Intrinsic culinary prominence in dish composition — not Pairing Engine reasoning.

domain_exclusions.beverages: Juices, cider, wine, and fruit beverages — out of domain
domain_exclusions.note: Entities in these domains are cross-referenced by canonical ID only — never duplicated intrinsically.
domain_exclusions.prepared_foods: Desserts, candies, jams, preserves, and flavored products — not ingredient ontology

fruit.description: Canonical culinary fruit, berry, or fruit-derived ingredient with frozen intrinsic metadata.

fruit_category.description: Top-level fruit domain hub (single category in v1).

fruit_group.description: Canonical Culinary Group (Pomes, Citrus, Processed Fruits, etc.).

botanical_ownership_rule.note: Suite rule — applies when botanical lineage diverges from culinary identity.

culinary_form_ownership_rule.description: A processed fruit becomes a separate canonical ingredient only when it has an established, independent culinary identity.

functional_pairing_rule.note: Suite wine-pairing rule — not expected to affect Fruit canonical ownership; remains part of suite governance.

processing_ownership_rule.description: When processing creates a distinct canonical culinary ingredient versus a preparation state or alias.

schema.taxonomy_note: Groups represent culinary identity and culinary form, not botanical families. See docs/FRUIT_GOVERNANCE.md.
